name: Parsley and Tabouleh
recipeIngredient: Grated lemon zest and juice of 1 lemon (or more to taste)
1/3 cup (or more to taste) extra virgin olive oil
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Cover bulgur wheat with cold water, about an inch deep, and soak for 30 minutes.
2. In a food processor, mince the parsley until you have 2 cups chopped.
3. Mince the mint leaves until you have 1/2 cup chopped.
4. Cut the chives into 1/2-inch pieces.
5. Drain the bulgur wheat and transfer it to a clean cloth or double layer of cheesecloth.
6. Wring out excess water; the grains should be moist and tender, not watery.
7. Transfer the drained bulgur to a bowl.
8. Add the chopped parsley, mint, and chives.
9. Stir in the lemon zest, juice, and olive oil.
10. Toss well to combine and season with salt and pepper as desired.
11. Adjust seasoning with more lemon zest, juice, or olive oil if needed.
12. Serve over lettuce leaves or use as a bed for halibut; top with diced tomatoes.
Yield: 4 servings
